title: cymbopogon nardus var. refractus
intro: Cymbopogon nardus var. refractus, commonly known as East Indian lemon grass, is a variety of the Cymbopogon nardus plant, a type of grass native to Southeast Asia and Australia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are derived from its essential oils. The health benefits of East Indian lemon grass include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be used in aromatherapy, topical applications, and internal consumption.
